title: Modals of Ability
description: Use can, could, and be able to accurately to express present, past, and positional ability in academic writing.
lesson_content_markdown:
# Modals of Ability

## Expressing Capability in Academic Writing

Modals of ability allow writers to describe what researchers, models, systems, and participants **can do** at different points in time. Selecting the correct form — **can**, **could**, or **be able to** — is determined by tense, grammatical position, and whether the ability refers to a general capacity or a specific successful achievement.

---

## Core Forms

| Modal | Use | Academic Example |
|---|---|---|
| **can** | Present ability; general truths | *Statistical software **can** detect patterns invisible to the human eye.* |
| **could** | Past ability (general capacity) | *Early models **could** process only limited datasets.* |
| **was/were able to** | Specific past achievement (success on one occasion) | *The team **was able to** complete data collection ahead of schedule.* |
| **be able to** | Infinitive & perfect forms; passive voice | *Researchers hope **to be able to** replicate these results.* |

---

## The Could vs. Was Able To Distinction

In **positive statements**, this distinction is subtle but significant:

- **Could** = general past capacity: *The original algorithm could analyse 1,000 records per second.*
- **Was/were able to** = a specific successful instance: *Despite the hardware failure, the team was able to recover all data.*

In **negative statements**, both are broadly interchangeable: *The model couldn't / wasn't able to generalise beyond the training set.*

---

## Grammatical Positions Requiring Be Able To

Use **be able to** (not *can*) after:
- another modal: *The system should **be able to** self-correct.*
- the infinitive particle: *Researchers hope **to be able to** replicate the findings.*
- perfect auxiliaries: *The team has not yet **been able to** secure funding.*

mastery_check.prompt_to_student: Select the most appropriate modal of ability for each academic sentence.

questions.question: Before the advent of high-performance computing, researchers ___ process only relatively small datasets within a reasonable timeframe.
Despite significant logistical challenges, the field research team ___ complete all 240 interviews within the allocated six-week period.
Modern neuroimaging technology ___ detect structural differences in the brain that were previously undetectable using standard clinical methods.
The research consortium hopes to ___ expand the longitudinal study into additional demographic cohorts in the coming years.
The institution has not yet ___ secure the external funding required for the third phase of the project.
